name: codex-theme-studio
title: Codex Theme Studio
job_to_be_done: Turn a user's brand system, native theme export, annotated screenshots, and optional artwork into a self-contained, reversible, visually verified theme for the official Codex Desktop app on macOS.

trigger_surface.description: Design, generate visual assets for, safely install, verify, iterate, and restore custom themes for the official Codex Desktop app on macOS. Use when a user asks to redesign Codex UI, create/apply/inject/replace a Codex skin or theme, adapt Codex to a brand system, generate a Codex banner or page background, repair defects in an injected theme, or roll back a custom theme. Do not use for general Codex health/config debugging, ordinary website theming, Windows/Linux, or direct app.asar modification.
should_trigger: Redesign or repair an injected Codex Desktop theme.
Adapt Codex to a brand system and preserve rollback.
Generate a Codex Banner or task-page background as part of a theme.
Pause or restore a custom Codex theme.
should_not_trigger: General Codex health or configuration debugging.
Any direct app.asar or signed app-bundle modification.
edge_cases: Native codex-theme-v1 palette export without CSS injection.
Read-only architecture analysis of a theme repository.
A visual mockup with no live theme installation.

steps: Collect the brand source, native theme export, screenshots, target viewport, optional artwork, and placement intent.
Separate native-product behavior from theme-owned visual decisions.
Use built-in ImageGen only when a new raster asset is needed; copy accepted project-bound output into the prepared theme directory.
Build a self-contained theme and run payload plus deterministic contract checks before any live change.
Install without launching, preserve immutable recovery state, and obtain explicit authorization before restarting a running Codex app.
Verify home, task, and New Task transition states; iterate one defect class at a time.
Hand off theme source, evidence, backup location, and exact pause and restore commands.
decision_points: Use artPlacement=hero by default; use all only when task-page readability is intentionally designed and verified.
Keep logos and existing vector systems code-native; use ImageGen for raster creation or editing.
Do not infer restart authorization from a request to design or install a theme.
Stop before mutation when app identity, loopback ownership, input safety, payload checks, or backups fail.
failure_modes: Theme selectors hide native tabs, cards, controls, or focus states.
Banner artwork duplicates live UI text or crops poorly at another width.
A task-page background reduces long-form reading contrast.
A generated image remains referenced from an ephemeral generator directory.
A running app is restarted without explicit authorization.
Recovery evidence is overwritten, incomplete, or confused with the active theme.
A foreign or remote DevTools target receives injected code.
Unavailable live, ImageGen, viewport, or human evidence is reported as passed.
